sections.paragraph: an unused root; Arab. اَهِبَ Eth. ለሀበ፡ to burn, to flame. The primary idea is that of licking, lapping, an idea which is found in roots beginning with the syllables להּ, לח, לע, and which is applied in various senses (see the root לֹועַ), namely, to a flame, which seems to lap. like a tongue, [“lambent tongues of flame”]; see לָהַט , and compare γλῶσσα πύρος, Act 2:3 [but this refers to the “other tongues” with which the Holy Ghost enabled the Apostles to testify to Jesus risen from the dead]. Whence-
